title: Rivian R1S and R1T Demand Trends 2024-2025
text: In 2024, Rivian delivered 51,579 combined R1S and R1T models, showing solid performance for the company's initial vehicle offerings. In 2025, the year has been significantly more challenging for Rivian's consumer vehicles. Global deliveries reached 42,247 vehicles in 2025, down 18% from 51,579 in 2024. Total 2025 Rivian R1 sales were 38,019, with R1S sales totaling 24,852 and R1T sales at 11,085. The most notable declines occurred in Q2 2025, where combined R1 consumer vehicle deliveries fell 31% year-on-year to 7,898 units. The R1T pickup saw a steep 47.1% drop to 1,752 units, while the R1S SUV slipped 24.5% to 6,146 units. The R1S ranked 8th among US EV sales in 2025. Demand for the high-end R1T and R1S vehicles has hit a ceiling. Rivian's struggles to grow R1S and R1T sales underscore just how critical next year's launch of the R2 SUV will be, as the company looks to a more affordable mainstream vehicle to drive future growth.
